items.title: [负面] The game is incredibly fun and the references to c...
items.summary:
The game is incredibly fun and the references to classics of the cosmic horror genre are mostly great! It's a shame that some of those references include all the racism. 

The story was quite good until chapter 7, at which point it introduces a desert civilization who the protagonists are constantly calling "primitive" who turn out to be one massive sex cult around the lovecraftian horror whose name contains a racial slur.

items.title: [正面] A really good roguelike gacha-deckbuilding game in...
items.summary:
A really good roguelike gacha-deckbuilding game inspired by the works and universe of H.P. Lovecraft. Love the music, the art and the themes. :)

I played a year ago but the English translation wasn't that great but now it is way better! Good job the Dev Team and the translators!

items.title: [正面] It's a good game. Lotsa options, and configuration...
items.summary:
It's a good game. Lotsa options, and configurations for deck building.
Plenty of challenges and thinking in this rogue light, but a lotta fun.

Don't recommend following the meta though, if you aren't whaling, you're probably gonna waste a lot of time on resources which you don't really  need or want, which will just ruin the game for you. All characters are useable, even the SR's, just depends on what type of teams you add them in.

It's a PVE game, enjoy it as such, just don't be afraid to try something new.

****Pros****:
--The story builds up and does get better the further you go into it. Lotta lore to gobble up.

--Each Awakener (characters) is unique and all have various roles they can play. 
This makes for an almost infinite amount of decks that can be made, that can fit almost any playstyle.

--Generous pull amounts overall that are free for both limited and standard. So it's pretty f2p friendly.

--Slow build game, if you are a patient player, you can get and build everything with enough time.

--Since it is a roguelite system, you can go back to previous challenges and feel way over powered compared to when you first did an area the first time, which i admit, will at least put a smile on your face. It also means that something that is challenging today, may become easy or very easy depending what you do with your characters / decks and add on's for them. The progression feel, is noticeable in this game, cause it can be a huge spike at times, depending on what you do (even something as simple as changing a character or wheel, will have noticeable affects).

****Cons****:
--Dupe system. This game requires more dupes to build characters or wheels (think equipment) than any other gacha i have ever played. Not by a couple either, by 4 or more compared to most other gacha's, so be aware. Though to be fair, typically 3 dupes makes any character much easier to use, and unlocks all but the level increases and special additions to the exalt skill (ultimate).

--Challenge added based on Keeper's (player) level. You are penalized for being below recommended level, and it takes an almost unfair amount of time to level your Keeper, especially after the 40's. There's a lota time gates due to this, which IMO are largely unneeded, as higher levels can take days and even weeks to go up a single level. As an example, you can't finish building one of the free SR's until level 70, where they release an upgrade on that level, & every two levels there after.

--Reset items for standard characters. While they are given for free periodically, they have a limited time to use them, and they can't be earned any other way other than when the Dev's "feel like it". Considering the above dupe issue i mentioned before, this is a negative. This is mainly due to when doing a reset you get a choice item for other standard banner characters to build them up. This is too infrequent to obtain and doesn't have something you can hold onto until you are ready to use it, since it's a use within a few days or it disappears.
